compiled_state.title: [validate-check-runners/test-batched]: bash tests/run-all.sh always interrupted after 45s -> validate.sh --ci stuck in infinite PENDING loop
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Infrastructure Failure

### Expected Behavior
`validate.sh --ci` should incrementally run tests across multiple invocations, eventually showing 'tests: PASS' once all tests complete.

### Actual Behavior
`validate.sh --ci` shows 'tests: PENDING' permanently. Every invocation records the test as 'interrupted' and the next run retries it, creating an infinite loop.

### Root Cause
`validate-check-runners.sh` passes `bash tests/run-all.sh` to `test-batched.sh` without `--runner=bash --test-dir=`. This triggers the generic runner, which treats the entire command as a single test with TOTAL=1. The command exceeds the 45s budget, is marked 'interrupted', and on resume the interrupted entry is filtered out of COMPLETED_LIST (line 415-418 of test-batched.sh), causing it to be retried forever.

The bash runner (which supports incremental per-file execution) is never activated because no `--test-dir` is provided.

### Evidence
- test-session-state.json: `{"runner": "bash tests/run-all.sh", "completed": ["bash_testsrun-allsh"], "results": {"bash_testsrun-allsh": "interrupted"}}`
- test-batched.sh line 415-418: interrupted entries are filtered from completed list on resume
- run-all.sh runs 6 suites (175+379+0+98+10 test files) concurrently — always exceeds 45s
